# Multiphase VOF cases (interFoam family, Foundation v10)

Free-surface / two-phase cases (dam break, sloshing, waves, filling) with
`interFoam`, `compressibleInterFoam`, `multiphaseInterFoam`. The single most
reliable move: retrieve the v10 `damBreak` tutorial with `find_similar_case`
(domain `multiphase`, category `damBreak`) and follow its structure — but the
rules below are what make it work.

## File inventory (differs from single-phase)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `constant/g` | REQUIRED — gravity vector (`uniformDimensionedVectorField`, e.g. `value (0 -9.81 0);`) |
| `constant/phaseProperties` | `phases (water air);` and `sigma 0.07;` (surface tension) |
| `constant/physicalProperties.water` | per-phase: `viscosityModel constant; nu 1e-06; rho 1000;` |
| `constant/physicalProperties.air` | per-phase: `nu 1.48e-05; rho 1;` |
| `constant/momentumTransport` | `simulationType laminar;` (or RAS) |
| `0/alpha.water` | phase fraction field, dimensions `[0 0 0 0 0 0 0]` |
| `0/p_rgh` | the pressure field VOF solvers use — there is NO `0/p` |
| `system/setFieldsDict` | initializes the liquid region (`boxToCell` + `volScalarFieldValue alpha.water 1`) |

Do NOT generate ESI-style multiphase files (`constant/transportProperties`
with phase sub-dictionaries, `cAlpha` in fvSolution) — that is the pre-v10/ESI
convention and a common training-prior trap.

## Field initialization

Write `0/alpha.water` with `internalField uniform 0;`, then run `setFields`
in Allrun (after mesh generation, before the solver). `setFieldsDict`'s
`defaultFieldValues` resets the field on every run, so this is idempotent
under `run_case`'s clean-rerun behavior — no `.orig` copy dance needed.

## Numerics that differ from single-phase

- **fvSchemes**: v10 puts interface compression in the divergence scheme:
  `div(phi,alpha) Gauss interfaceCompression vanLeer 1;` (pre-v10/ESI used a
  `cAlpha` entry in fvSolution instead — do not use that). Momentum flux is
  `div(rhoPhi,U)` (not `div(phi,U)`).
- **fvSolution `"alpha.water.*"` entry**: MULES settings —
  `nAlphaCorr 2; nAlphaSubCycles 1; MULESCorr yes; nLimiterIter 5;` plus a
  smoothSolver block.
- **fvSolution solvers**: `p_rgh` + `p_rghFinal { $p_rgh; relTol 0; }` and a
  `pcorr` (or `"pcorr.*"`) entry; PIMPLE typically
  `momentumPredictor no; nOuterCorrectors 1; nCorrectors 3;`.
- **controlDict**: `adjustTimeStep yes; maxCo 1; maxAlphaCo 1;` with a small
  initial `deltaT` (e.g. 0.001), and `writeControl adjustableRunTime;` so
  writes land on round times.

## Boundary condition idioms

- Walls: `p_rgh` → `fixedFluxPressure`, `alpha.water` → `zeroGradient`,
  `U` → `noSlip`.
- Open top ("atmosphere"): `p_rgh` → `totalPressure` (v10; `p0 uniform 0`),
  `U` → `pressureInletOutletVelocity`, `alpha.water` → `inletOutlet`
  (`inletValue uniform 0`).

## Transient success checks (after run_case says "success")

Read the solver log tail and verify:

- final `Time =` equals `endTime` (a mid-run crash can still leave logs);
- `Phase-1 volume fraction` stays constant across the run (mass conservation);
- `Min(alpha.water)` ≥ ~-1e-6 and `Max` ≤ 1 (MULES boundedness);
- cumulative continuity errors stay small;
- time directories exist via `list_case_files`.
